jokes.text: like Meredith or Kevin. I mean, who's gonna give Kevin an award? Dunkin Donuts?
jokes.setup: Michael explaining who doesn't get awards very often
jokes.punchline: Implying Kevin's only achievement would be eating donuts
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific insult that reveals Michael's casual cruelty while being genuinely funny. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: Neighbor's hanged himself, due to lack of recognition.
jokes.setup: Michael's elaborate story about why employee recognition is important
jokes.punchline: Taking the metaphor to an absurdly dark conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Michael's tone-deaf nature and tendency to escalate stories inappropriately. Dark humor that's shocking but character-driven.

jokes.text: The Dundies are like a car wreck that you want to look away but you have to stare at it because your boss is making you.
jokes.setup: Comparing Dundies to car wreck you can't look away from
jokes.punchline: The twist that it's forced workplace attendance, not morbid curiosity
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with a clever subversion of the car wreck metaphor. Captures the workplace comedy premise perfectly.

jokes.text: It's fat Halpert
jokes.setup: Michael looking at past Dundies footage
jokes.punchline: Casual, cruel nickname for Jim
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective cringe moment showing Michael's inappropriate commentary. High character integration.

jokes.text: Oh I can't, because I keep them hidden, I don't wanna look at them and get cocky.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Jim to show off his Dundies
jokes.punchline: Jim's deadpan sarcastic response about getting 'cocky' over joke awards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim response - dry, sarcastic, and dismissive of Michael's manufactured importance. Great character voice.

jokes.text: And mine are at home, in a display case above my bed.
jokes.setup: Jim's sarcastic comment about hiding his awards
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely serious revelation about his sad shrine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Michael's pathetic pride. The visual of a Dundies shrine above his bed is perfectly sad.

jokes.text: TMI, TMI my friend. TMI? Too Much Information. It's just easier to say TMI. I used to say 'Don't Go There' but that's lame.
jokes.setup: Michael saying TMI to Jim
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining the acronym and his evolution of slang usage
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael over-explanation that reveals his desperate attempt to seem current. Loses points for verbosity.

jokes.text: I don't know where they are, I think I threw them out. - Oh, no you didn't. - I think I did.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Stanley to show his Dundies
jokes.punchline: Stanley's complete indifference and Michael's disbelief
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley moment - completely unimpressed with Michael's 'awards'. Good contrast between characters.

jokes.text: To Oscar Martinez, It's the 'Show Me The Money' award!
jokes.setup: Footage from previous Dundies
jokes.punchline: Generic award name with no connection to Oscar
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's lazy approach to awards - just using movie quotes. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: A little bit of Pam, all night long. A little bit of Angela, on the sing.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Dwight to perform
jokes.punchline: Dwight's terrible singing performance with inappropriate lyrics
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight awkwardness. The cringe factor works but it's not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: This is the part where Kevin stands in front of the camcorder all night. It's great.
jokes.setup: Watching old Dundies footage
jokes.punchline: Kevin accidentally blocking the camera the entire time
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that's very Kevin - oblivious and unintentionally disruptive. Jim's deadpan delivery helps.

jokes.text: That was just something that was written on the ladies room wall.
jokes.setup: Dwight demanding to know what's written about Michael
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's bathroom graffiti about their boss
jokes.explanation: Sets up the bathroom graffiti subplot. Functional setup more than a standalone joke.

jokes.text: Will her highness Jan Levinson Gould be descending from her corporate throne this evening to visit us lowly serfs here, Dunder Mifflin Scranton?
jokes.setup: Michael asking Jan about attending the Dundies
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic medieval metaphor for corporate hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Good Michael pompousness, though a bit verbose. The medieval metaphor is fitting for his ego.

jokes.text: Well, you could take the bus, you could work on the way here, sleep on the way home.
jokes.setup: Jan saying it's too far to drive
jokes.punchline: Michael's ridiculous bus suggestion for a corporate executive
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's complete lack of understanding of corporate culture and Jan's status.

jokes.text: You're dropping an A-bomb on me here! - Really? I'm dropping an atomic bomb on you.
jokes.setup: Michael using 'A-bomb' metaphor
jokes.punchline: Jan's literal interpretation and sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good interplay showing Jan's exasperation with Michael's dramatics. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: No reasons? It was the 05-05-05 party! - It happens once every billion years!
jokes.setup: Jan questioning Michael's previous party
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely wrong math about date significance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - completely wrong but delivered with total confidence. The absurd timeframe is perfect.

jokes.text: Ok, no that was a fun raiser, I think I made that very clear on the flyers. Fun, F-U-N.
jokes.setup: Jan mentioning the tsunami fundraiser lost money
jokes.punchline: Michael's 'fun raiser' wordplay defense
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael trying to lawyer his way out with wordplay. The spelling out shows his desperation.

jokes.text: It's a little character I like to do. It is loosely based on Karnack, one of Carson's classic characters.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his comedy bit
jokes.punchline: His pretentious explanation of a hack impression
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's delusions about his comedy skills and need to reference 'classic' entertainment.

jokes.text: We got... the PLO, the IRA and the hot-dogs stand behind the warehouse. Name 3 businesses that have better healthcare plans than Dunder Mifflin.
jokes.setup: Michael doing his Carnac impression
jokes.punchline: Comparing company healthcare to terrorist organizations and a hot dog stand
jokes.explanation: Clever dark humor that works on multiple levels - bad healthcare, inappropriate references, and the hot dog stand escalation.

jokes.text: Here's the problem: There is no open bar because of Jan, and it's the reason why comedy clubs have a 2-drink minimum.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the party problems
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding why comedy clubs have drink minimums
jokes.explanation: Good Michael logic - he thinks drunk audiences are necessary for comedy rather than just revenue generation.

jokes.text: Dwight get out of here what are you doing in the ladies room...? - You are a pervert!
jokes.setup: Dwight caught in the ladies room
jokes.punchline: Everyone assuming the worst about Dwight
jokes.explanation: Pays off the bathroom graffiti setup. Dwight's overzealousness leads to embarrassment.

jokes.text: The Dundee award, for longest engagement, goes to Pam Beesly... When will that girl gonna be married? That's what I have to say
jokes.setup: Giving Pam an award for longest engagement
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate commentary about her personal life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael cruelty disguised as an award. Highlights the Jim-Pam-Roy dynamic painfully.

jokes.text: We'll see you next year! - Yeah! Oh nuts! Oh gosh!
jokes.setup: Roy accepting Pam's engagement award
jokes.punchline: Roy's casual dismissal and Michael's awkward reaction
jokes.explanation: Roy's casualness about postponing marriage again is perfectly uncomfortable. Michael's reaction shows even he realizes it went too far.

jokes.text: That's why it's funny, every year that Roy and Pam don't get married it gets funnier!
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting the joke is getting old
jokes.punchline: Michael doubling down on the cruel 'humor'
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete lack of empathy on display. His logic is perfectly wrong and cruel.

jokes.text: Having a bathroom is a privilege, it is called a ladies-room for a reason, and if you cannot behave like ladies, well then you are not going to have a bathroom.
jokes.setup: Dwight addressing the bathroom graffiti
jokes.punchline: Threatening to take away the women's bathroom entirely
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight authoritarianism. The absurd punishment and formal delivery make this excellent character comedy.

jokes.text: We are going to have 2 men rooms.
jokes.setup: Dwight taking away the ladies room
jokes.punchline: His illogical solution of two men's rooms
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that perfectly captures Dwight's illogical authoritarianism. Concise and absurd.

jokes.text: Don't don't talk don't touch stop it oddment!
jokes.setup: Dwight continuing his bathroom rant
jokes.punchline: Michael's panicked, incoherent attempt to stop him
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's panic when Dwight goes too far. The word salad 'oddment' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: how there is no money, and how there is no food, and how the jokes are really bad.
jokes.setup: Michael addressing rumors about the Dundies
jokes.punchline: Accidentally listing accurate criticisms
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke where Michael accidentally admits all the problems while trying to dismiss rumors.

jokes.text: Keep your acceptance speeches short, I have wrap it up music and I'm not afraid to use it, Devin.
jokes.setup: Michael giving standard award show instructions
jokes.punchline: Singling out Devin specifically and threateningly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's petty authority. The specific threat to Devin is funny.

jokes.text: The Dundies are kind of like a kid's birthday party, and you go, and there's really nothing for you to do there, but the kid's having a really good time, so, you're kind of there. That's kind of what it's like.
jokes.setup: Pam describing the Dundies experience
jokes.punchline: Perfect analogy comparing Michael to a happy child at his own party
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures the dynamic. Sweet but pointed, and extremely quotable.

jokes.text: please, please, do not drink and drive, because you may hit a bump, and spill the drink.
jokes.setup: Standard responsible drinking PSA opening
jokes.punchline: Subverting safety concern to worry about spilling alcohol
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect structure. Classic Michael priorities - completely wrong but delivered confidently.

jokes.text: I did, and why didn't you Stanley? - I did, my wife's name is Terry. - Well, I'm looking forward to meeting Terry. - It's this person whose hand I'm holding Michael.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about bringing family
jokes.punchline: Michael completely oblivious to Stanley's wife right next to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael obliviousness. Stanley's deadpan correction makes it even better.

jokes.text: I was out on a very very hot date last night with a girl from HR Dwight. - Really? We don't have any girls from HR. - I know that, for the sake of the story.
jokes.setup: Michael starting a story about dating someone from HR
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal fact-check and Michael's meta acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Great character interplay. Dwight's literal-mindedness vs Michael's storytelling, with a meta twist.

jokes.text: When she had me fill out six hours worth of paper work. - Like an AIDS test? - No... god.
jokes.setup: Michael's joke about HR paperwork in bed
jokes.punchline: Dwight's completely inappropriate and literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Dwight's inability to understand metaphor creates uncomfortable comedy. Michael's horror is perfect.

jokes.text: This says bushiest beaver. - What? I told them busiest... idiots!
jokes.setup: Phyllis receiving the 'Busiest Beaver' award
jokes.punchline: The sexual double entendre was printed instead of the intended word
jokes.explanation: Classic misprint humor with sexual undertones. Michael's genuine shock sells it.

jokes.text: Herro everybodeeee.
jokes.setup: Michael doing a racist impression
jokes.punchline: His casual racism as 'entertainment'
jokes.explanation: Offensive and lazy humor that shows Michael's tone-deafness. Only works as character exposition, not as comedy.

jokes.text: Ryan the temp!
jokes.setup: Giving the 'hottest in office' award
jokes.punchline: Giving it to the young male temp, revealing Michael's attraction
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation. The uncomfortable subtext combined with Michael's obliviousness is perfect.

jokes.text: What am I going to do with the award? Nothing, I don't know what I'm gonna do. That's the least of my concerns right now.
jokes.setup: Ryan receiving the inappropriate award
jokes.punchline: His complete discomfort and dismissal of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing Ryan's horror at the situation. His prioritizing bigger concerns is relatable.

jokes.text: The Tight Ass award goes to Angela, not only because she is everybody's favorite stickler, but because she has a great caboose!
jokes.setup: Giving Angela an award about being uptight
jokes.punchline: Adding inappropriate sexual commentary
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael sexual harassment disguised as awards. Angela's predictable 'No' reaction expected.

jokes.text: I think those might be empty. - No, no 'cause the ice melts, and then it's like second drink!
jokes.setup: Someone noting Pam's drinks might be empty
jokes.punchline: Pam's drunk logic about melted ice becoming a drink
jokes.explanation: Good drunk logic from Pam. Shows her getting progressively more intoxicated in a believable way.

jokes.text: Spicy curry, what's that mean? - Not everything means something, this is a joke. - Yeah, but why did you give it to me? - This is a bowler! - I know! They didn't have any businessmen so...
jokes.setup: Kelly questioning her racist award
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive non-explanation and cheap trophy excuse
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's casual racism and inability to explain his 'humor.' Kelly's reasonable questioning makes it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I got Dwight, sucking the funny out of room
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on his performance
jokes.punchline: Blaming Dwight for his own bad comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael deflection - can't admit his jokes are bad, must be someone else's fault.

jokes.text: We has came from your mama's house.
jokes.setup: Michael asking where hecklers are from
jokes.punchline: Classic juvenile comeback that embarrasses Michael
jokes.explanation: Basic 'your mom' joke that works mainly to show Michael getting heckled by teenagers.

jokes.text: this is the 'don't go in there after me' award, it's for the time that I went to the bathroom after him and it was really, really smelly
jokes.setup: Michael giving Kevin a bathroom-related award
jokes.punchline: Extremely personal and inappropriate award about Kevin's bowel movements
jokes.explanation: Juvenile bathroom humor that's more cruel than funny. Shows Michael's lack of boundaries.

jokes.text: Well, last year I got great work, so I don't know... I don't know what to think about this award
jokes.setup: Stanley getting 'fine work' award after 'great work' last year
jokes.punchline: Stanley's subtle complaint about being downgraded
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley moment - dry observation about Michael's arbitrary award system. Great character voice.

jokes.text: It is the whitest sneakers award, because she always has the whitest tennis shoes on!
jokes.setup: Pam getting an award about her shoes
jokes.punchline: Extremely mundane and pointless award category
jokes.explanation: Shows how desperate Michael is for award categories. Mundane observation passed off as meaningful.

jokes.text: Ok, first off, my Keds, because I couldn't have done it without them, thank you.
jokes.setup: Pam giving acceptance speech for shoe award
jokes.punchline: Playing along with the ridiculous award by thanking her shoes
jokes.explanation: Great drunk Pam moment. Playing along with the absurdity while being genuinely funny.

jokes.text: Finally I want to thank God, because God gave me this Dundee, and I feel God in this Chili's tonight.
jokes.setup: Pam continuing her acceptance speech
jokes.punchline: Treating a joke award as divine intervention in a chain restaurant
jokes.explanation: Iconic moment. Perfect escalation from shoe award to religious experience. The Chili's setting makes it even funnier. Became a cultural reference.

jokes.text: And we heard Michael change the lyrics to a number of classic songs, which for me, has ruined them for life!
jokes.setup: Jim recapping the evening's events
jokes.punchline: The permanent damage Michael's song parodies have caused
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim observation about the lasting trauma of Michael's 'entertainment.' Great callback that ties the episode together.

jokes.text: So I killed, almost!
jokes.setup: Michael saying he made Pam laugh so hard she almost broke her neck
jokes.punchline: His proud pun about 'killing' in comedy vs. literally almost killing someone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - turning accidental injury into comedy success. Perfect character moment with great economy.

jokes.text: Oh my god! I just wanna say that this was the best Dundies ever!
jokes.setup: Pam being escorted out after falling
jokes.punchline: Her genuine enthusiasm for the disaster of an evening
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing drunk Pam's genuine appreciation. The irony of calling it the 'best' after all the disasters is perfect.

jokes.text: Apparently this young woman was sneaking drinks off other people's tables.
jokes.setup: Manager explaining why Pam is banned
jokes.punchline: Revealing how Pam got so drunk without an open bar
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the no-open-bar setup. Shows Pam's resourcefulness and explains her intoxication.

jokes.text: I feel bad about what I wrote on the bathroom wall. - No you don't.
jokes.setup: Kelly expressing guilt about the bathroom graffiti
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate, accurate dismissal of her fake remorse
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Jim's blunt honesty and Kelly's transparent fake guilt work perfectly together.

jokes.text: I just wanted to say thanks. - That's not really a question.
jokes.setup: Pam asking if she can ask Jim a question
jokes.punchline: Jim's literal correction when she just says thanks
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with typical Jim precision. Shows their comfortable dynamic even when Pam's drunk.

jokes.text: Yes that too but I mean with the audio, great work!
jokes.setup: Michael being thanked for checking Pam's condition
jokes.punchline: Michael's confusion, thinking he's being praised for his comedy performance
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment showing his ego and confusion about what he's being complimented for.
